arts.ttl: Arsenal 'target' £75m Premier League striker amid honest transfer admission
arts.sum: football.london says: Arsenal were heavily linked with signing a striker during the summer, and it would come as no surprise if the position is still an area in need of strengthening ahead of the New Year. football.london says: Kimmich was tentatively linked with a move to Arsenal during the summer, but the majority of the reports have always suggested that Barcelona would be his most likely destination if he was to leave Bayern.

arts.ttl: Arsenal 1-0 Shakhtar Donetsk: Player ratings as wasteful Gunners secure nervy win
arts.sum: Leandro Trossard missed a penalty after the restart as Shakhtar threatened to stun the hosts with a late leveller, but the Gunners held on to secure an important win before Sunday's Premier League battle with Liverpool. The Gunners found themselves ahead at half-time thanks to a slice of luck following Shakhtar goalkeeper Dmytro Riznyk's own goal and they could have extended their lead before the break had it not been for some wasteful finishing.

arts.ttl: Erling Haaland may ruin Arsenal transfer plan as major twist comes to light
arts.sum: Manchester City star Erling Haaland is being targeted by Real Madrid and Sporting striker Viktor Gyokeres has been linked with a move to the Etihad to replace the Norwegian

arts.ttl: Arsenal and Chelsea on red alert as John Henry and FSG make clever £325m Liverpool decision
arts.sum: The decision by Liverpool's owners Fenway Sports Group and principal owner John Henry is expected to bring the Merseyside club's kit earnings into line with Arsenal, Manchester City and Chelsea, who all receive about £60million-£65million from deals with Adidas, Puma and Nike respectively, The Guardian reports. Arsenal's kit contract with Adidas runs until 2030 and is said to be worth around £600million.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta handed another huge Arsenal injury concern before Liverpool clash
arts.sum: Arsenal's injury crisis continues to go from bad to worse for Mikel Arteta after Riccardo Calafiori was subbed off in the second half of his side's Champions League clash against Shakhtar Donetsk. Jurrien Timber, Bukayo Saka, and Martin Odegaard all missed the clash at Emirates Stadium with doubt cast over the trio for this weekend's Premier League clash against Liverpool.

arts.ttl: Oleksandr Zinchenko claims Pep Guardiola called him after Man City won the Treble and thanked him for his contributions at the club - despite defender playing the season at Arsenal
arts.sum: Man City made history in 2022-23 as they claimed the Premier League, FA Cup and Champions League - becoming just the second English side to win the Treble.

arts.ttl: Arsenal's Oleksandr Zinchenko hands his shirt to a Shakhtar Donetsk fan after 1-0 win - as the Ukrainian defender receives a warm welcome from the travelling supporters amid his nation's ongoing war with Russia
arts.sum: Oleksandr Zinchenko shared a heartwarming moment with Shakhtar Donetsk's fans after Arsenal beat the Ukrainian side 1-0 at the Emirates on Tuesday night.

arts.ttl: Why Gabriel Magalhaes shouted at Gabriel Jesus as Arsenal make statement vs Shakhtar Donetsk
arts.sum: The Emirates Stadium atmosphere has certainly taken something of a beating by many supporters, particularly on European nights, with the revamped version of the Champions League seemingly having an impact on the feeling of weight inside the ground. Leandro Trossard would miss a penalty to set up a nervy finish to the match with many players absent and Riccardo Calafiori going off injured.

arts.ttl: Arsenal player ratings vs Shakhtar Donetsk as Martinelli shines but Jesus off it
arts.sum: Arsenal started the match against Shakhtar Donetsk brightly and could have been in front inside the first five minutes through Riccardo Calafiori. The opening goal just after the half hour mark was befitting of the low quality affair with Gabriel Martinelli's post-hitting shot bouncing into the net off Dmytro Riznyk's backside.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta delivers Riccardo Calafiori and Bukayo Saka Arsenal injury update in Liverpool crisis
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has labelled Bukayo Saka as unlikely to play against Liverpool on Sunday after he was left out of the matchday squad for Tuesday's 1-0 Champions League win over Shakhtar Donetsk. With the visit of Liverpool on Sunday to come for Arsenal, as they look to bounce back from a disappointing 2-0 defeat to Bournemouth at the weekend, Arteta is uncertain if either Calafiori or Saka will be available.
